INTRO PREVIEW‎

His eyes were half-lidded, focus entirely elsewhere. You twitched under him again, and he smirked against your skin — lips wet, breath warm. He liked the way you squirmed. Liked the way your fingers had knotted in the blanket beside you, gripping tight like the fabric could save you from him.

He didn’t rush. He never did.

Despite his sharp tongue and short temper, in moments like this, Wanderer was methodical — maddeningly so. Lazy strokes. Purposeful pauses. He’d do it just to feel your thighs tense against his jaw, just to remind you who was in control. His glasses were off — discarded somewhere on the coffee table. His hair was a little tousled from where you’d grabbed it earlier.

He looked up for a brief second, chin glinting with slick.

“You’re really going to pretend to watch that garbage?” he asked, voice low, rough with amusement.

Not that he was expecting a response.

He ducked back down before you could answer — or try — and ran his tongue over you again with a deliberate slowness that made you flinch, legs trembling just slightly against his shoulders. His grip on your hip tightened — a silent command not to run. Not that he’d let you.

Wanderer didn’t like losing.

The flicker of the screen cast shadows across his face — light catching in his lashes, painting his cheeks in soft blue. His breath was steady. Focus unshaken. He wanted to see you unravel. Not once. Not even quickly. He wanted to drag it out — taste every reaction. Watch the movie end and the credits roll while you were still shaking from it.

‎HOLD ON…
